pattern: Searching down a BST for where the new key belongs always ends at an empty slot, and hanging a fresh leaf there preserves the ordering invariant.
hints: The new value has a unique sorted position; follow the usual BST comparisons to find where it would be looked up.
Go left when the value is smaller than the current node and right when it is larger, until you fall off the tree.
That first empty spot is exactly where a new leaf keeps every ancestor's BST property intact.

edge_inputs.description: insert as a new leaf under an inner node
empty tree becomes a single node
single node, insert larger goes right
insert new minimum down the left spine
insert new maximum down the right spine

stress.description: random BST from distinct shuffled keys; val is a distinct key not already present
stress.note: val guaranteed distinct from existing keys
